name: Berlinerkranzer/Betty Crocker
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Mix butter, sugar, orange peel, and eggs thoroughly until well combined.
3. Gradually blend in flour until a smooth dough forms.
4. Shape the dough into rounded teaspoonfuls and roll each into a 6-inch rope about 1/4-inch in diameter.
5. Form each rope into a circle, then bring one end over and through to create a "wreath" with the ends extending 1/2 inch beyond the circle.
6. Place the cookies on an ungreased baking sheet.
7. Beat egg white until foamy, then add sugar tablespoon by tablespoon while continuing to beat.
8. Brush the tops of the cookies with beaten egg white.
9. Press tiny bits of chopped candied cherries into the center of each wreath, using red for holly berries and green for leaves.
10. Bake for 10-12 minutes until set but not brown. Remove from sheets immediately.
These freeze well for several months if well-wrapped. I always store them separately because other cookies tend to pick up their orange scent/flavor. Betty Crocker.
